 1. Write the packet_id (uint32_t) using the stream's operator<<.
 2. Write the username (std::string) with a length prefix. According to the reference, the operator<< for std::string uses a fixed-length prefix (prefixed). So we can directly use stream << packet.username.
 3. Write the scores vector with a length prefix. We can use the prefixed adaptor for the vector. The reference shows that writing a container with prefixed will write the size as a uint32_t in little-endian and then the elements.
 4. Write the metadata string as null-terminated. We can use the null_terminated adaptor for the metadata string.

 However, note that the reference material shows that the operator<< for std::string already uses a fixed-length prefix. But for the metadata, we want null-terminated. The reference provides a null_terminated adaptor for strings.

 Also, note that the scores vector is a container of uint32_t. The reference material shows that when we use the prefixed adaptor for a container, it will write the count (as uint32_t) and then each element. Since uint32_t is an arithmetic type, each element will be written using the POD overload (which writes the raw bytes).

 Implementation of the serialise method:

   void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << packet_id;
        stream << username; // This will use the prefixed string serialization

        stream << hexi::null_terminated(metadata); // Use null_terminated adaptor for the metadata

     void read_container(container_type& container, const count_type count) {

         if constexpr(!memcpy_read<container_type, binary_stream_reader>) {

```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;

    void serialise(auto& adaptor) {
        if constexpr (std::is_same_v<std::decay_t<decltype(adaptor)>, hexi::Stream>) {
            // Serialization
            adaptor.write(sensor_id);
            // Write the size of temperatures
            auto count = static_cast<uint32_t>(temperatures.size());
            auto converted_count = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse(count, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
            adaptor.write(converted_count);
            for (auto& temp : temperatures) {
                auto converted = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse(temp, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
                adaptor.write(converted);
            }
            auto converted_pressure = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse(pressure, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
            adaptor.write(converted_pressure);
        } else {
            // Deserialization
            adaptor.read(sensor_id);
            // Read the size of temperatures
            uint32_t count;
            adaptor.read(count);
            hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(count);
            temperatures.resize(count);
            for (auto& temp : temperatures) {
                float value;
                adaptor.read(value);
                hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(value);
                temp = value;
            }
            double p;
            adaptor.read(p);
            hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(p);
            pressure = p;
        }
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_measurement_data(const MeasurementData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::Stream stream(buffer);
    stream.serialise(data);
    return buffer;
}

MeasurementData deserialize_measurement_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    MeasurementData data;
    hexi::Stream stream(buffer);
    stream.deserialise(data);
    return data;
}

struct SensorInfo {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::string_view sensor_name;
};

SensorInfo extract_sensor_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    SensorInfo info;
    hexi::Stream stream(buffer);
    // Use view to extract sensor name (if present)
    info.sensor_name = stream.view();
    // Check if there's enough data for sensor_id
    stream.enforce_read_bounds(4);
    // Read sensor_id
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    stream.read(&sensor_id);
    info.sensor_id = sensor_id;
    // Skip the remaining data
    stream.skip(stream.size());
    return info;
}
```
